reports.description: Typed, versioned definition of a multi-step automation. Compiles to a stable execution graph the runtime can replay from trace_entrys.
reports.trust_boundary: plugin-authored static definition; loaded read-only at runtime; never model-authored during a run

reports.description: Machine-readable catalog of reusable flow blocks. Names each block, its input and output contracts, evidence, check, allowed routes, and host-specific human-decision handling.
reports.trust_boundary: repo-authored static catalog. Shape is validated by src/schemas/flow-blocks.ts and tests/contracts/flow-block-catalog.test.ts.

reports.description: Authoring-layer schematic shape for assembling reusable flow blocks into runnable compiled flows. Captures schematic steps, stage bindings, execution labels, typed evidence contracts, contract aliases, named route targets, and per-depth route_overrides. Compiled to generated/flows/<id>/circuit.json (and per-mode <mode>.json siblings) by src/runtime/compile-schematic-to-flow.ts at build time.
reports.trust_boundary: repo-authored static schematic. Shape is validated by src/schemas/flow-schematic.ts and tests/contracts/flow-schematic.test.ts.

reports.description: Atomic unit of execution inside a Stage. Four variants (compose, verification, checkpoint, relay) discriminated by kind.
reports.trust_boundary: plugin-authored static definition; loaded read-only at runtime

reports.description: Named, ordered grouping of Steps within a CompiledFlow. May align with canonical stage path or be flow-specific.
readers: circuit-next engine (stage advancement, stage path enforcement)
reports.trust_boundary: plugin-authored static definition; loaded read-only at runtime

reports.description: Append-only sequence of trace_entrys for a single Run. First trace_entry is bootstrap; sequence is 0-based contiguous; closure is singleton with no post-closure trace_entrys.
writers: circuit-next engine (trace_entry append during run execution)
reports.trust_boundary: operator-local persisted state; append-only; never truncated in normal operation

reports.description: In-memory {log, snapshot} aggregate used for internal consistency checks. Not persisted. The log persists at run.trace (trace.ndjson); the snapshot persists at run.snapshot (state.json). RunProjection exists only to state the invariants that bind the two in memory (trace_entries_consumed equals log length; bootstrap-frozen fields match; closure-to-status mapping is total by construction).
reports.trust_boundary: engine-computed in-memory aggregate; always recomputable from run.trace; never authoritative over the log; never persisted to disk (Slice 26a binding split — run.snapshot owns state.json)

reports.description: Reducer-derived run-local state checkpoint persisted at <circuit-next-run-folder>/state.json. Always recomputable from run.trace by replaying trace_entrys; never independently authored. Does NOT include the trace itself — for the in-memory {log, snapshot} aggregate see run.projection. Slice 27c lands the reducer-derived writer and byte-match check.
writers: circuit-next engine (reducer-derived snapshot writer; authored by Slice 27c)
reports.trust_boundary: operator-local derived state; state.json is written only by the reducer-derived snapshot writer at src/runtime/snapshot-writer.ts (never by step executors). A byte-match against a fresh reducer pass over run.trace is the re-entry check. Slice 27c lands the writer; re-entry byte-match enforcement in resume lands in Slice 27d+ per plan.

reports.description: Byte-for-byte copy of the flow manifest taken at run bootstrap, persisted at <circuit-next-run-folder>/manifest.snapshot.json. Carries the exact persisted manifest bytes (base64-encoded for JSON transport) plus a SHA-256 hash over those raw bytes. The byte-match re-entry check rejects any manifest drift between bootstrap and resume. Slice 27c authors this report and its writer.
writers: circuit-next engine (manifest snapshot writer; authored by Slice 27c)
reports.trust_boundary: operator-local persisted state; written once at bootstrap by the manifest snapshot writer at src/runtime/manifest-snapshot-writer.ts. Hash algorithm: SHA-256 over the exact persisted manifest snapshot bytes (`algorithm: 'sha256-raw'`), per ADR-0001 Addendum B §Stage 1.5 Close Criteria #8. Parse-time superRefine rejects any snapshot whose declared hash disagrees with sha256 over decoded bytes_base64; a second reader cannot be tricked into accepting a tampered byte-body under the declared hash.

reports.description: User-visible summary of what a run produced, persisted at <circuit-next-run-folder>/reports/result.json once the closing run.closed trace_entry is appended. Unlike state.json (reducer-derived, recomputable from run.trace) a RunResult is written once at close and never mutated; it is the authoritative 'what happened' report independent of future log rewrites. Slice 27c declares the shape; Slice 27d wires the writer into runtime-proof.
writers: circuit-next engine (result writer; wired by Slice 27d runtime-proof)
reports.trust_boundary: operator-local persisted state; written by the engine at run.closed append. `outcome` must agree with the closing run.closed trace_entry's outcome and `run_id`/`flow_id` must agree with the run.bootstrapped trace_entry (runtime-enforced at write time); the schema enforces shape only.

reports.description: Typed override value (model, effort, skills, depth, invocation_options) contributed by one config layer. JSON-safe invocation_options; strict unknown-key rejection.
writers: operator (user-global/project live; invocation seam pending public CLI flags)
backing_paths: invocation arguments (ephemeral, in-memory; resolver-supported, public CLI flags pending)
reports.trust_boundary: operator-local at user-global/project layers and future invocation-layer projection; plugin-author-signed at the defaults layer; engine validates .strict() key closure and ghost-provenance rejection at parse time. Composition produces selection.resolution (separate report).

reports.description: Result of layering overrides under 7-tuple precedence. Identity-keyed applied chain; category-level precedence; ghost-provenance rejection.
backing_paths: in-memory at relay resolution time; <circuit-next-run-folder>/trace.ndjson persists the RelayStartedTraceEntry.resolved_selection projection
reports.trust_boundary: engine-computed; always reconstructable from ordered overrides

reports.description: Config-layer map of custom connector name -> CustomConnectorDescriptor. Registry-key to descriptor-name parity is enforced; reserved names are disjoint.
reports.trust_boundary: operator-local at user-global/project/invocation layers; plugin-author-signed at the defaults layer; engine validates reserved-name disjointness, own-property closure, and registry-key/descriptor-name parity at parse time.

reports.description: Pre-resolution pointer into the connector registry or a built-in connector. Three variants (built-in, named, inline custom). Not valid in the trace.
reports.trust_boundary: operator-local at user-global/project/invocation layers; plugin-author-signed at the defaults layer; engine validates named-ref registry closure during resolution. MUST NOT appear in run.trace (resolved-form-only per connector-I10).

reports.description: Post-resolution connector: either a built-in enum variant or an inlined CustomConnectorDescriptor. Named references MUST NOT appear here. Shape written to RelayStartedTraceEntry.connector.
reports.trust_boundary: engine-computed; authoritative for relay; replay-stable (named refs forbidden by connector-I10)

reports.description: Top-level Config record contributed by one config layer. Carries schema_version (literal 1), a RelayConfig (sub-report connector.registry), a record of per-circuit CircuitOverride (sub-report config.circuit-override), and a defaults object carrying a SelectionOverride. Surplus keys rejected transitively via .strict().
writers: operator (user-global/project live; invocation layer pending public CLI flags)
backing_paths: invocation arguments (ephemeral, in-memory; public CLI flags pending)
dangerous_sinks: surplus-key rejection at config root and nested defaults
reports.trust_boundary: operator-local at user-global/project/invocation layers; plugin-author-signed at the defaults layer; engine validates .strict() key closure + schema_version literal at parse time. Composition of multiple layers is out of v0.1 scope (see docs/contracts/config.md Scope).

reports.description: Layer-identity wrapper around a Config. Carries ConfigLayer (default | user-global | project | invocation), an optional source_path, and a validated Config. Slice 86 product discovery emits user-global/project; default and invocation are resolver-supported injection seams until later product wiring. Distinct from the merged effective configuration at relay time (which is not a LayeredConfig).
backing_paths: in-memory wrapper around config.root (no standalone on-disk form)
reports.trust_boundary: engine-computed wrapper around an already-validated config.root; each layer's inner config is re-validated through config.root before wrapping. source_path is advisory (v0.1 does not constrain filesystem validity).

reports.description: Per-flow override slot stored in Config.circuits[flow_id]. Currently carries an optional SelectionOverride; reserved for additional per-circuit override categories in v0.2. Strict-key rejection forbids smuggling new categories without a contract change.
writers: operator (user-global/project live; invocation seam pending public CLI flags)
backing_paths: invocation arguments (ephemeral, in-memory; public CLI flags pending)
dangerous_sinks: strict-key rejection (forbids `skills: string[]` v0.0 shortcut reintroduction)
reports.trust_boundary: operator-local at user-global/project/invocation layers; plugin-author-signed at the defaults layer. Record-key shape (CompiledFlowId) is validated by ids scalar; catalog-closure of the CompiledFlowId against installed flows is NOT enforced at v0.1 (same posture as connector.registry closure notes).

reports.description: circuit-next catalog entry for a plugin skill's L1 frontmatter. Represents the machine-readable descriptor a selection resolver can bind by SkillId and a catalog compiler can enumerate. Prose body (L2) and extra files (L3) are out of scope.
readers: circuit-next engine (selection resolver, skill set composition)
backing_paths: <plugin>/catalog/skills.json (compiled catalog projection, one SkillDescriptor per skill)
reports.trust_boundary: plugin-authored static descriptor; loaded read-only at catalog-compile time; never model-authored during a run

reports.description: circuit-next continuity handoff record. Carries goal, next, state, and debt narrative across session boundaries; identity derived from record_id which is used as a filename stem.
reports.trust_boundary: operator-local persisted state; may contain model-authored narrative and path-derived identity
reports.migration_policy: deferred; no transparent runtime parse of old Circuit records. If imported later, a separate migration-source contract is required.

reports.description: circuit-next continuity resolver/index report. Points at the pending record and the current attached run; determines which record is authoritative for resume. Singleton on disk — no in-body identity.
reports.trust_boundary: operator-local persisted state; determines which record is authoritative for resume
reports.migration_policy: deferred; no transparent runtime parse of old Circuit index. If imported later, a separate migration-source contract is required.

reports.description: Framing report produced by the Frame stage of an explore flow run. Declares the subject, operator task statement, and success condition for the investigation. Slice 89 starts P2.10 by binding this report to a strict schema and default runtime writer.
dangerous_sinks: success-condition binding for synthesize + review stages
reports.trust_boundary: engine-authored deterministic v0 writer during Frame stage; read-only thereafter within the run; not cross-run shared. A future model-authored framing writer must reopen this trust-boundary text.
reports.migration_policy: Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference Markdown report, accepted for CC#P2-1 by ADR-0007 Slice 99; no transparent parse of legacy brief.md is attempted.

reports.description: Analyze-stage report produced by an explore flow run. Decomposes the subject (from explore.brief) into named aspects with evidence citations. Slice 89 starts P2.10 by binding this report to a strict schema and default runtime writer.
dangerous_sinks: compose input — aspects that don't round-trip cleanly will leak into the compose recommendation without trace
reports.trust_boundary: engine-authored deterministic v0 writer during Analyze stage from explore.brief; read-only thereafter within the run. A future model-authored analysis writer must reopen this trust-boundary text.
reports.migration_policy: Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference Markdown report, accepted for CC#P2-1 by ADR-0007 Slice 99; no transparent parse of legacy analysis.md is attempted.

reports.description: Synthesize-stage report — the explore flow's primary output. A recommendation, decision candidate set, or investigation conclusion with explicit mapping back to the brief's success condition. Slice 90 binds this relay-produced report to a strict schema.
writers: explore synthesize-step (worker relay, role=implementer per ADR-0008)
dangerous_sinks: relay-result-to-report materialization (per ADR-0008 §Decision.3a) — the canonical compose.json value is produced by schema-parsing the connector-authored writes.result payload, so an adversarial or malformed connector response is the upstream risk surface that downstream readers inherit
reports.trust_boundary: model-authored during Synthesize stage via connector relay (worker-relay step with role=implementer per ADR-0008); the role tag binds to RelayConfig.roles.implementer at runtime, recorded in the five-trace_entry relay transcript with payload + receipt + result hashes per ADR-0007 CC#P2-2 + §Amendment (Slice 37); distinct-connector / distinct-model enforcement is evidential at P2.4/P2.5 landing (weaker-evidence disclosure per ADR-0007 §6.4; Codex Slice 38 HIGH 1 fold-in — RelayConfig.roles permits implementer and reviewer to bind to the same connector at v0.2, and ResolvedConnector carries no differs-from-prior-relay field); canonical report materializes from writes.result via ADR-0008 §Decision.3a rule; read-only thereafter within the run; the primary output report for explore flows
reports.migration_policy: Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference Markdown decide outputs, accepted for CC#P2-1 by ADR-0007 Slice 99; no transparent parse of legacy plan.md or decision.md is attempted.

reports.description: Review-stage report produced by an explore flow run. Adversarial pass over explore.compose with verdict, objections, and missed-angle enumeration. Slice 91 binds this relay-produced report to a strict schema.
writers: explore review-step (worker relay, role=reviewer per ADR-0008)
dangerous_sinks: close-stage result composition — the review verdict is the primary signal the operator sees at close

relay-result-to-report materialization (per ADR-0008 §Decision.3a) — the canonical review-verdict.json value is produced by schema-parsing the connector-authored writes.result payload; an adversarial reviewer-connector could produce a verdict that over-accepts or over-rejects, and the close-stage aggregation inherits that risk
reports.trust_boundary: model-authored during Review stage via connector relay (worker-relay step with role=reviewer per ADR-0008); the role tag binds to RelayConfig.roles.reviewer at runtime, recorded in the five-trace_entry relay transcript with payload + receipt + result hashes per ADR-0007 CC#P2-2 + §Amendment (Slice 37); distinct-connector / distinct-model separation from the implementer-role connector is evidential at P2.4/P2.5 landing (weaker-evidence disclosure per ADR-0007 §6.4; Codex Slice 38 HIGH 1 fold-in — RelayConfig.roles.reviewer and RelayConfig.roles.implementer may bind to the same connector at v0.2, so the Knight-Leveson boundary is a relay-machinery-routing precondition at v0.2 that becomes contract-guaranteed at P2.4/P2.5 config/parity enforcement); canonical verdict report materializes from writes.result via ADR-0008 §Decision.3a rule; read-only thereafter; adversarial by design

reports.description: Close-stage aggregate report for the explore flow run. Composes a deterministic summary, verdict snapshot, and pointers to the four prior reports (brief, analysis, compose, review-verdict). Persisted at <run-folder>/reports/explore-result.json — distinct from the engine-authored universal run.result at <run-folder>/reports/result.json (per-flow path split). Per-flow <kind>-result.json siblings is the canonical pattern for future flow-specific aggregates (build-result.json, fix-result.json, etc.).
dangerous_sinks: run result consumer; operator-facing summary of the explore run
reports.trust_boundary: engine-computed at Close by the registered explore.result@v1 compose writer from schema-parsed compose.json and review-verdict.json plus flow-declared evidence link paths; terminal report — no in-run readers; cross-run reader is the run result consumer only; path-distinct from run.result so the engine's result-writer (src/runtime/result-writer.ts RESULT-I1 — single writer to result.json) and the orchestrator's close-step (flow-semantic aggregate at explore-result.json) do not collide
reports.migration_policy: Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference Markdown close report, accepted for CC#P2-1 by ADR-0007 Slice 99; no transparent parse of legacy result.md is attempted.

reports.description: Frame checkpoint brief for the Build flow. Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference Build brief.md; captures objective, scope, success criteria, verification command candidates, and checkpoint request/response pointers.
dangerous_sinks: success criteria and verification command candidate binding for later Build stages
reports.trust_boundary: engine-computed at Frame checkpoint from the operator-local goal and declared checkpoint policy; read-only after checkpoint response is recorded
reports.migration_policy: Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference Markdown report; no transparent parse of legacy brief.md is attempted.

reports.description: Plan-stage report for the Build flow. Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference Build plan.md; carries approach, planned slices, and direct-argv verification commands for the later verification step.
reports.trust_boundary: engine-computed at Plan from schema-parsed build.brief at v0; future model-authored planning must preserve the direct-argv verification command contract
reports.migration_policy: Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference Markdown report; no transparent parse of legacy plan.md is attempted.

reports.description: Act-stage implementation report for the Build flow. Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference implementation handoff/result surface; summarizes changed files and implementation evidence returned by the worker.
reports.trust_boundary: model-authored during the Build Act worker relay; schema-parsed before downstream verification, review, and close readers consume it
reports.migration_policy: Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference Markdown report; no transparent parse of legacy implementation-handoff.md is attempted.

reports.description: Verify-stage evidence report for the Build flow. Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference verification.md; records each executed verification command and the aggregate pass/fail status.
reports.trust_boundary: engine-computed by direct argv verification command execution from schema-parsed build.plan commands; read-only after write
reports.migration_policy: Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference Markdown report; no transparent parse of legacy verification.md is attempted.

reports.description: Review-stage report for the Build flow. Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference review.md; records independent reviewer verdict, summary, and findings after verification evidence exists.
reports.trust_boundary: model-authored during the Build Review worker relay after build.verification exists; schema-parsed before close consumes it
reports.migration_policy: Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference Markdown report; no transparent parse of legacy review.md is attempted.

reports.description: Close-stage aggregate report for the Build flow. Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference result.md; summarizes the run outcome and points to the five prior Build reports. Persisted at <run-folder>/reports/build-result.json, distinct from the universal run.result at <run-folder>/reports/result.json.
reports.trust_boundary: engine-computed at Close from schema-parsed Build role reports; path-distinct from the universal engine-computed run.result
reports.migration_policy: Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference Markdown report; no transparent parse of legacy result.md is attempted.

reports.description: Frame report for the Fix schematic. Clean-break structured JSON successor informed by the old Repair brief; captures the concrete problem, expected behavior, observed behavior, scope, success criteria, and verification command candidates.
dangerous_sinks: success criteria and verification candidate binding
reports.trust_boundary: engine-computed or model-authored at Fix Frame from the operator-local goal; schema-parsed before downstream schematic steps consume it
reports.migration_policy: Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference Markdown report; no transparent parse of legacy brief.md is attempted.

reports.description: Gather Context report for the Fix schematic. Records sources, observations, and open questions before diagnosis.
reports.trust_boundary: model-authored or engine-computed during Fix Gather Context; schema-parsed before diagnosis consumes it
reports.migration_policy: Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference Markdown report; no transparent parse of legacy analysis.md is attempted.

reports.description: Diagnose report for the Fix schematic. Records reproduction status, cause summary, confidence, evidence, and residual uncertainty.
reports.trust_boundary: model-authored during Fix Diagnose from schema-parsed brief and context; schema-parsed before action or close consumes it
reports.migration_policy: Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference Markdown report; no transparent parse of legacy analysis.md is attempted.

reports.description: Human Decision report for the Fix schematic when evidence is uncertain or the problem is not reproduced.
reports.trust_boundary: operator-local when answered by the operator, or engine-computed from declared mode policy when a safe default is used; schema-parsed before downstream items consume it
reports.migration_policy: Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference behavior; no transparent parse of legacy prompt text is attempted.

reports.description: Act report for the Fix schematic. Summarizes the focused change, linked diagnosis, changed files, and implementation evidence.
reports.trust_boundary: model-authored during Fix Act worker execution; schema-parsed before verification, review, and close consume it
reports.migration_policy: Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference Markdown report; no transparent parse of legacy implementation handoff is attempted.

reports.description: Run Verification report for the Fix schematic. Records executed proof commands and aggregate pass/fail status.
reports.trust_boundary: engine-computed by direct argv verification command execution from schema-parsed Fix proof inputs; read-only after write
reports.migration_policy: Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference Markdown report; no transparent parse of legacy verification.md is attempted.

reports.description: Review report for the Fix schematic. Records independent reviewer verdict, summary, and findings when the mode requires review.
reports.trust_boundary: model-authored during Fix Review after fix.verification exists; schema-parsed before close consumes it
reports.migration_policy: Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference Markdown report; no transparent parse of legacy review.md is attempted.

reports.description: Close report for the Fix schematic. Summarizes outcome, verification status, review verdict when present, residual risks, and pointers to Fix reports. Persisted at <run-folder>/reports/fix-result.json, distinct from the universal run.result at <run-folder>/reports/result.json.
reports.trust_boundary: engine-computed at Fix Close from schema-parsed Fix role reports; path-distinct from the universal engine-computed run.result
reports.migration_policy: Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference Markdown report; no transparent parse of legacy result.md is attempted.

reports.description: Close-stage primary report for the audit-only review flow. Structured JSON successor to the reference review.md report; aggregates reviewer findings and carries the deterministic final verdict.
dangerous_sinks: close-stage aggregation from model-authored analyze relay output
reports.trust_boundary: engine-computed at Close by the default registered review compose writer from a model-authored analyze-stage relay result; tests/runner/review-runtime-wiring.test.ts proves schema-valid review.result materialization on the default CLI path. Read-only after write.
reports.migration_policy: Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference Markdown report; no transparent parse of legacy review.md is attempted.

reports.description: Frame report for the Migrate flow. Names the migration's source and target, scope, success criteria, coexistence appetite, rollback plan, and the verification command candidates the post-batch Verify step will run. Synthesized at Frame from the run goal; a future operator-input slice can author it directly.
dangerous_sinks: scope binding for inventory + coexistence stages
verification command candidate selection for the post-batch Verify step
reports.trust_boundary: engine-computed at Frame from the operator-local goal via the registered migrate.brief@v1 compose writer; schema-parsed before downstream steps consume it; read-only thereafter within the run

reports.description: Inventory report for the Migrate flow. Enumerates the migration targets the runtime knows about (id, repository-relative path, category, description) and groups them into named batches the Execute stage will run. v0 produces a single batch covering all items; future fanout-over-batches will produce N batches.
dangerous_sinks: execute-stage batch relay — items missing from the inventory will not be migrated, and unknown ids referenced by a batch will fail the inventory shape check
reports.trust_boundary: engine-computed at Inventory from the migrate.brief; the v0 substrate fabricates from the goal and is the named substrate-decision deferred work tracked in operator memory; schema-parsed (with cross-field id integrity superRefine) before coexistence-step consumes it

reports.description: Coexistence Plan report for the Migrate flow. Captures the strategy for old + new running side-by-side while batches are in flight: switchover criteria, the observable signals that prove coexistence is healthy, the rollback path the operator can take at any cutover boundary, and the residual risks.
dangerous_sinks: rollback path commitment — the operator inherits the documented rollback as the survivable exit at every batch boundary
switchover criteria — review-stage admits cutover only when the criteria are met
reports.trust_boundary: engine-computed at Coexistence Plan from migrate.brief plus migrate.inventory; schema-parsed before execute-step consumes it; read-only thereafter within the run

reports.description: Batch Execution report for the Migrate flow. The Execute stage delegates the actual code change to a Build child flow via sub-run; the child's run.result is copied verbatim into the parent's writes.result slot. MigrateBatch re-exports RunResult so downstream readers (close-writer, tests) have a typed alias to parse against. The verdict on the child RunResult is what the parent's check.pass admits.
writers: migrate execute-step (sub-run handler — Build child flow)
dangerous_sinks: verify-stage input — the child's verdict checks whether Verify even runs
review-stage admission — cutover review reads batch evidence to decide cutover-approved vs cutover-blocked
reports.trust_boundary: produced by a Build child flow run via the sub-run handler; the child's own relay + verification + review trust boundaries apply to the materialized RunResult; the parent treats the result as immutable evidence

reports.description: Verify-stage report for the Migrate flow. Records executed proof commands and aggregate pass/fail status using the shared VerificationResult shape, namespaced as migrate.verification@v1 so the schematic's verify step can read against a migrate-specific contract without forcing every flow to re-author the verification shape.
dangerous_sinks: close-stage outcome — failed verification cannot land cutover-approved
reports.trust_boundary: engine-computed by direct argv verification command execution from schema-parsed Migrate brief candidates; read-only after write

reports.description: Cutover Review report for the Migrate flow. Adversarial pass over the migration evidence (brief, inventory, coexistence plan, batch result, verification) with a four-state verdict (cutover-approved | cutover-with-followups | cutover-blocked | reject), summary, and findings. Findings are required for any non-approved verdict.
writers: migrate review-step (worker relay, role=reviewer per ADR-0008)
dangerous_sinks: close-stage outcome composition — only cutover-approved or cutover-with-followups admit forward motion
relay-result-to-report materialization (per ADR-0008 §Decision.3a) — the canonical review.json value is produced by schema-parsing the connector-authored writes.result payload
reports.trust_boundary: model-authored during Review stage via connector relay (worker-relay step with role=reviewer per ADR-0008); the role tag binds to RelayConfig.roles.reviewer at runtime, recorded in the five-trace_entry relay transcript with payload + receipt + result hashes per ADR-0007 CC#P2-2; canonical verdict report materializes from writes.result via ADR-0008 §Decision.3a rule; read-only thereafter; adversarial by design

reports.description: Close-stage aggregate report for the Migrate flow. Composes a deterministic summary, outcome (complete | cutover-deferred | reverted | failed), verification status, review verdict, batch count, and pointers to the six prior reports (brief, inventory, coexistence, batch, verification, review). Persisted at <run-folder>/reports/migrate-result.json — distinct from the engine-authored universal run.result.
dangerous_sinks: run result consumer; operator-facing summary of the Migrate run
outcome reporting — cutover-deferred and reverted are first-class non-failure outcomes that the operator must distinguish from complete
reports.trust_boundary: engine-computed at Close by the registered migrate.result@v1 close builder from schema-parsed Migrate role reports plus flow-declared evidence link paths; terminal report — no in-run readers; cross-run reader is the run result consumer only; path-distinct from run.result

reports.description: Frame report for the Sweep flow. Names the sweep's objective, type (cleanup | quality | coverage | docs-sync), scope, success criteria, scope exclusions, out-of-scope items, high-risk boundaries that need extra care, and the verification command candidates the post-batch Verify step will run.
dangerous_sinks: scope and exclusion binding for analyze + queue stages
high-risk boundary signaling for the batch executor
verification command candidate selection for the post-batch Verify step
reports.trust_boundary: engine-computed at Frame from the operator-local goal via the registered sweep.brief@v1 compose writer; schema-parsed before downstream steps consume it; read-only thereafter within the run

reports.description: Analyze-stage report for the Sweep flow. Decomposes the scope into ranked cleanup candidates with id, category, repository-relative path, description, confidence (low | medium | high), and risk (low | medium | high). The accept verdict checks whether queue/triage runs at all.
writers: sweep analyze-step (worker relay, role=implementer per ADR-0008)
dangerous_sinks: queue triage input — candidates with overstated confidence will get queued for execution despite weak evidence

relay-result-to-report materialization (per ADR-0008 §Decision.3a) — the canonical analysis.json value is produced by schema-parsing the connector-authored writes.result payload, so an adversarial or malformed connector response is the upstream risk surface that downstream readers inherit
reports.trust_boundary: model-authored during Analyze stage via connector relay (worker-relay step with role=implementer per ADR-0008); the role tag binds to RelayConfig.roles.implementer at runtime, recorded in the five-trace_entry relay transcript with payload + receipt + result hashes per ADR-0007 CC#P2-2; canonical analysis report materializes from writes.result via ADR-0008 §Decision.3a rule; cross-validated by sweep.queue@v1 against the candidate id set in the queue-stage

reports.description: Triage report for the Sweep flow. Classifies each analyzed candidate into one of four actions (act | prove-then-act | prove | defer) with a per-item rationale, then partitions the classified set into to_execute and deferred lists. The schema enforces that to_execute and deferred reference only classified candidate_ids, and that no id appears in both partitions.
dangerous_sinks: execute-stage batch relay — only candidates in to_execute are run; the rest are deferred and surfaced via deferred_count in sweep.result
cross-report validator — sweep.queue@v1 is verified against sweep.analysis@v1's candidate id set
reports.trust_boundary: engine-computed at Queue/Triage from sweep.analysis with cross-field validation (no unknown candidate_ids, no double-classification); schema-parsed before execute-step consumes it

reports.description: Batch Execution report for the Sweep flow. Records per-item status (acted | reverted | partial) plus evidence, the changed file set, and a derived verdict (accept | partial | reverted). The schema's superRefine asserts the verdict is consistent with the observed item statuses (all reverted → reverted, any reverted or no acted → partial, otherwise accept).
writers: sweep execute-step (worker relay, role=implementer per ADR-0008)
dangerous_sinks: verify-stage input — failed items in the batch can still pass overall verdict if the partial/accept rule is satisfied
review-stage injection check — the reviewer reads batch + verification together
reports.trust_boundary: model-authored during Execute via connector relay (worker-relay step with role=implementer per ADR-0008); cross-validated against the to_execute set declared by sweep.queue@v1; canonical batch report materializes from writes.result via ADR-0008 §Decision.3a rule; read-only thereafter

reports.description: Verify-stage report for the Sweep flow. Records executed proof commands and aggregate pass/fail status using the shared VerificationResult shape, namespaced as sweep.verification@v1 so the schematic's verify step can read against a sweep-specific contract without forcing every flow to re-author the verification shape.
dangerous_sinks: review-stage evidence — failed verification cannot land a clean review verdict
close-stage outcome — verification_status feeds the SweepResult.outcome
reports.trust_boundary: engine-computed by direct argv verification command execution from schema-parsed Sweep brief candidates; read-only after write

reports.description: Review report for the Sweep flow. Adversarial pass over the sweep evidence with a four-state verdict (clean | minor-injections | critical-injections | reject), summary, and findings. The Sweep review's central concern is whether the cleanup introduced any regressions or scope creep beyond what the brief authorized — findings are required for any non-clean verdict.
writers: sweep review-step (worker relay, role=reviewer per ADR-0008)
dangerous_sinks: close-stage outcome composition — critical-injections and reject force a non-complete outcome
reports.trust_boundary: model-authored during Review stage via connector relay (worker-relay step with role=reviewer per ADR-0008); the role tag binds to RelayConfig.roles.reviewer at runtime, recorded in the five-trace_entry relay transcript with payload + receipt + result hashes per ADR-0007 CC#P2-2; canonical verdict report materializes from writes.result via ADR-0008 §Decision.3a rule; read-only thereafter; adversarial by design

reports.description: Close-stage aggregate report for the Sweep flow. Composes a deterministic summary, outcome (complete | partial | reverted | failed), verification status, review verdict, deferred candidate count, and pointers to the six prior reports (brief, analysis, queue, batch, verification, review). Persisted at <run-folder>/reports/sweep-result.json — distinct from the engine-authored universal run.result.
dangerous_sinks: run result consumer; operator-facing summary of the Sweep run
deferred_count surface — communicates which candidates were intentionally not executed so the operator can re-queue them in a follow-up sweep
reports.trust_boundary: engine-computed at Close by the registered sweep.result@v1 close builder from schema-parsed Sweep role reports plus flow-declared evidence link paths; terminal report — no in-run readers; cross-run reader is the run result consumer only; path-distinct from run.result
